sanskrit: श्री भगवानुवाचहन्त ते कथयिष्यामि दिव्या ह्यात्मविभूतयः । प्राधान्यतः कुरुश्रेष्ठ नास्त्यन्तो विस्तरस्य मे ॥
hindiTranslation: श्रीभगवान् बोले  हाँ, ठीक है ।  मैं अपनी दिव्य विभूतियोंको तेरे लिये प्रधानतासे संक्षेपसे कहूँगा क्योंकि हे कुरुश्रेष्ठ  मेरी विभूतियोंके विस्तारका अन्त नहीं है ॥
englishTranslation: Krishna said: I will tell you briefly of my divine manifestations, chief of them, Arjuna, for there is no end to their विस्तार.
essence: The divine can be named briefly, never exhausted.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: Krishna agrees to describe his chief divine manifestations, but only in brief, because his full extent has no limit.
simpleInsight: There is comfort in knowing the divine presence cannot be exhausted or fully measured.
detailedMeaning: On Kurukshetra, Arjuna keeps asking Krishna to explain how to remember him in daily life. Krishna now answers by promising a selective list of his chief manifestations, while making clear that his full greatness cannot be contained in any list.
modernRelevance: You ask someone to explain what keeps them steady through chaos, and they give you a few examples — then admit the real answer is bigger than any summary.
translationLiteral: श्रीभगवानुवाच (the Blessed Lord said) / हन्त (indeed) / ते (to you) / कथयिष्यामि (I will tell) / दिव्याः (divine) / हि (indeed) / आत्मविभूतयः (my own manifestations) / प्राधान्यतः (chiefly / in the main) / कुरुश्रेष्ठ (O best of the Kurus) / नास्ति (there is not) / अन्तः (end) / विस्तरस्य (of the विस्तार / spread) / मे (of me)
